task_scores.task_title: Phase 1 — Backend Mock Data Layer (mock_welders, generate_frames_for_arc, prototype)
task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: Frame params (angle/amps drift) produce scores outside PASS_BANDS; prototype exits 1 and seeding proceeds with wrong-score arcs, so badges and trends mislead demo viewers.
task_scores.reasoning: Foundation for all other phases; high leverage, explicit file paths and verification; no blockers.

task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: Partial seed (existing == expected_count with corrupt operator_id) skips re-seed; demo shows wrong welder names or orphan sessions that wipe does not remove.
task_scores.reasoning: Critical path for demo; idempotent logic and spot-check documented; depends on Phase 1.

task_scores.task_title: Phase 3 — Dashboard (10 welder cards, On track / Needs attention badges)
task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: 20 fetchScore calls with no timeout cause indefinite hang; one 404 cascades and blocks other cards if not using Promise.allSettled.
task_scores.higher_leverage_alternative: Batch GET /api/scores?ids=... would reduce 20 calls to 1; plan notes OOS.
task_scores.reasoning: Primary demo surface; 5s timeout and allSettled mitigate fetch risks; clear verification criteria.

task_scores.task_title: Phase 4 — Welder Report (historical scores, trend chart, expert comparison, last-slot fix)
task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: When last historical fetch fails, using 0 instead of sc.total makes generateAIFeedback infer false 'declining' trend for improving welders.
task_scores.reasoning: Adversarial critique already addressed last-slot fix; explicit code and verification; depends on seed.

task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: Prototype not in CI (test_prototype_arc_scoring_exits_zero missing or flaky) allows score-band drift; refactor breaks arcs and demo silently.
task_scores.reasoning: Lower strategic value than deliverable phases but essential for data-integrity contract; prototype-in-CI mitigates high risk.

critical_issues: Session ID migration (sess_expert_001 → sess_expert-benchmark_005) — any bookmark, script, or frontend expecting legacy IDs will 404; plan documents this but does not list concrete files to update.
Prototype pre-flight: if prototype_arc_scoring.py exits 1, seeding Phase 2 produces wrong-score data; pre-flight checklist exists but is easy to skip in a 16h sprint.

Step 4.4 chartData bug: code uses histResults for chartData value but for last slot when hist fails it should use sc.total — verification says historicalScores[last] = sc.total but chartData still uses 0 for failed slots (chart shows 0; trend uses sc — verify both paths are consistent).
minor_issues: WELDERS duplicated in page.tsx and welder/[id]/page.tsx; 11th archetype requires two edits.
Orphan sessions: wipe does not remove sess_* from removed archetypes; manual DELETE documented but easy to forget.

Step 5.3 last-slot test: 'or backend test that asserts generateAIFeedback behavior' — frontend test infra may be limited; backend test of generateAIFeedback with [72,75,78,80,0] vs [72,75,78,80,82] is not yet specified.
suggestions: Add explicit grep/list step in pre-flight: 'Find all references to sess_expert_001 and sess_novice_001' before Phase 2 deploy.
In Step 4.4 chartData construction, when i === lastIdx and hist failed, use sc.total for chartData.value so chart and trend are consistent.
Add test_prototype_arc_scoring_exits_zero to CI config (e.g. .github/workflows) so it runs on every PR, not only when pytest is invoked.
dependency_order: T1 before T2 (seed needs generate_session_for_welder); T2 before T3 and T4 (dashboard/welder report need seeded sessions); T3 and T4 can run in parallel after T2; T5 depends on T1–T4 (tests assert implemented behavior).
premortem_top_risk: Prototype exits 1 (or is skipped) before Phase 2; seed runs with frame params that miss PASS_BANDS; demo shows wrong badges and trends with no visible error.
goal_alignment_notes: The five phases together serve the enterprise-demo goal well: visible 10-card dashboard, badges, and welder report within 16h. All deliverables are demo-visible within 48h (assuming seed + dev server). Critical fixes (last-slot trend, prototype in CI, idempotent seed) address prior critique; dependency order is clear.
